segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: When launching a new business, entrepreneurs face countless decisions from choosing a target market to deciding which features to include in their first product.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: These choices can be overwhelming, especially when resources are limited and time is short.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: To address these challenges, some startups adopt a strategy that emphasizes speed, experimentation, and customer feedback.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: This approach, the lean startup methodology, was popularized by Eric Ries and has gained traction in the tech world and beyond.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: One of its core practices is building a minimum viable product, or MVP.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: This is a basic version of a new product that can be released quickly to gather feedback from early users.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: The goal is to learn what works and what doesn't, so that future development is governed by actual user experience rather than assumptions.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Another key principle is validated learning.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Instead of doing traditional market research, like surveys or focus groups, entrepreneurs test their ideas by putting the product out in the real world.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: This helps them figure out whether their product actually meets the needs of their customers.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: In all fairness, I should point out that this approach isn't perfect.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Rapid iteration and constant feedback can sometimes lead to confusion or a lack of clear direction, and in the pursuit of efficiency some teams may cut corners, which could affect product quality.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Still, many entrepreneurs find the lean startup methodology helpful for navigating the uncertainties of launching a new business.
